steps.title: Identify the side length
steps.content:
A cube with side length $s$ has surface area $6s^2$. Compare that with the given surface area.
$$6s^2=6(\frac{k}{4})^2$$
$$s=\frac{k}{4}$$

steps.title: Find the face perimeter
steps.content:
One face is a square, so its perimeter is $4$ times the side length.
$$4(\frac{k}{4})=k$$
The answer is B.
